words.def: to stop feeling angry with somebody who has done something to harm, annoy or upset you; to stop feeling angry with yourself
words.example: forgive somebody, Can you ever forgive me?

words.def: the general arrangement, plan, design, etc. of something
words.example: The format of the new quiz show has proved popular.

words.def: the action of forming something; the process of being formed
words.example: the formation of a new government

words.def: that existed in earlier times
words.example: This fine ruin was, in former times, a royal castle.

words.def: in the past
words.example: Namibia, formerly known as South West Africa

words.def: having or bringing an advantage, an opportunity, a piece of good luck, etc.
words.example: Remember those less fortunate than yourselves.

words.def: a large amount of money
words.example: He made a fortune in real estate.

words.def: an event or medium where people can exchange opinions and ideas on a particular issue; a meeting organized for this purpose
words.example: forum (on something), to hold an international forum on drug abuse

words.def: directed or moving towards the front
words.example: The door opened, blocking his forward movement.

words.def: the parts of a dead animal or a plant that have become hard and turned into rock
words.example: fossils over two million years old

words.def: to start something, such as an organization or an institution, especially by providing money
words.example: to found a club/company/school

words.def: a principle, an idea or a fact that something is based on and that it grows from
words.example: Respect and friendship provide a solid foundation for marriage.

words.def: a person who starts an organization, institution, etc. or causes something to be built
words.example: the founder and president of the company

words.def: a small part or amount of something
words.example: Only a small fraction of a bank's total deposits will be withdrawn at any one time.

words.def: a small part of something that has broken off or comes from something larger
words.example: fragment (of something), Police found fragments of glass near the scene.

words.def: the parts of a building or an object that support its weight and give it shape
words.example: built on a wooden framework

words.def: the crime of cheating somebody in order to get money or goods illegally
words.example: She was charged with credit card fraud.

words.def: without particular plans or arrangements; not busy
words.example: Are you free on Saturday? We're having a barbecue.

words.def: the power or right to do or say what you want without anyone stopping you
words.example: to defend/protect academic freedom

words.def: without anyone trying to prevent or control something
words.example: the country’s first freely elected president

words.def: the rate at which something happens or is repeated
words.example: Fatal road accidents have decreased in frequency over recent years.

words.def: happening or doing something often
words.example: He is a frequent visitor to this country.

words.def: behaving in a kind and pleasant way because you like somebody or want to help them
words.example: a warm and friendly person

words.def: any material that produces heat or power, usually when it is burnt
words.example: diesel/jet/rocket fuel

words.def: to do or achieve what was hoped for or expected
words.example: to fulfil your dream/ambition/potential

words.def: for all the hours of a week during which people normally work or study, rather than just for a part of it
words.example: students in full-time education

words.def: completely
words.example: I fully understand your motives.

words.def: a special activity or purpose of a person or thing
words.example: The club serves a useful function as a meeting place.

words.def: an amount of money that has been saved or has been made available for a particular purpose
words.example: a disaster relief fund

words.def: serious and very important; affecting the most central and important parts of something
words.example: the fundamental principles of scientific method

words.def: in every way that is important; completely
words.example: The two approaches are fundamentally different.

words.def: money for a particular purpose; the act of providing money for such a purpose
words.example: federal/state funding

words.def: very angry
words.example: Their incompetence made me furious.

words.def: in addition to what has just been stated. Furthermore is used especially to add a point to an argument.
words.example: He said he had not discussed the matter with her. Furthermore, he had not even contacted her.

words.def: an increase in the amount of something, especially in wealth or weight
words.example: The opposition made unexpected gains in the last election.

words.def: playing computer games
words.example: online/mobile/console gaming

words.def: an organized group of criminals
words.example: criminal gangs

words.def: sexually attracted to people of the same sex
words.example: gay men

words.def: the fact of being male or female, especially when considered with reference to social and cultural differences, rather than differences in biology; members of a particular gender as a group
words.example: issues of class, race and gender

words.def: a unit inside a cell that controls a particular quality in a living thing that has been passed on from its parents
words.example: a dominant/recessive gene

words.def: to produce energy, especially electricity
words.example: The wind turbines are used to generate electricity.

words.def: connected with genes (= the units in the cells of a living thing that control its physical characteristics) or genetics (= the study of genes)
words.example: genetic and environmental factors

words.def: unusually great intelligence, skill or artistic ability
words.example: the genius of Shakespeare

words.def: a particular type or style of literature, art, film or music that you can recognize because of its special features
words.example: literary/musical genres

words.def: real; exactly what it appears to be; not artificial
words.example: Is the painting a genuine Picasso?

words.def: truly; in a way that is exactly what it appears to be and is not artificial
words.example: There are some genuinely funny moments in the film.

words.def: a movement that you make with your hands, your head or your face to show a particular meaning
words.example: He made a rude gesture at the driver of the other car.

words.def: a performance by musicians playing popular music or jazz in front of an audience; a similar performance by a comedian
words.example: They're doing a gig in Boston tonight.

words.def: the world (used especially to emphasize its size)
words.example: tourists from every corner of the globe

words.def: the being or spirit that is worshipped and is believed to have created the universe
words.example: Do you believe in God?

words.def: made of gold
words.example: a golden crown

words.def: the quality of being good
words.example: the essential goodness of human nature

words.def: very beautiful and attractive; giving great pleasure
words.example: a gorgeous girl/man

words.def: to legally control a country or its people and be responsible for introducing new laws, organizing public services, etc.
words.example: The country is governed by elected representatives of the people.

words.def: a person who is the official head of a country or region that is governed by another country
words.example: the former governor of the colony

words.def: to take or hold somebody/something with your hand suddenly or roughly
words.example: grab somebody/something, She grabbed his arm as he got up to leave.

words.def: a mark given in an exam or for a piece of school work
words.example: (British English), She got good grades in her exams.

words.def: slowly, over a long period of time
words.example: to gradually increase/decrease

words.def: the small hard seeds of food plants such as wheat, rice, etc.; a single seed of such a plant
words.example: Russia sold 12 million tons of grain abroad last year.

words.def: impressive and large or important
words.example: It's not a very grand house.

words.def: a sum of money that is given by the government or by another organization to be used for a particular purpose
words.example: government/federal grants

words.def: connected with drawings and design, especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.
words.example: graphic design

words.def: designs, drawings or pictures that are used especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.
words.example: Text and graphics are prepared separately and then combined.
